behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at a module handoff boundary (collectors -> aggregator -> summary input), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: not required; source clients can be faked in-process because this behavior checks payload composition, not real network behavior. LLM-DEP: no claim about model output quality, only input completeness. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text is about "content passed" (handoff completeness), not real HTTP/OS effects; rejected Agentic/Workflow because it does not assert summary quality or sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/orchestration modules with three deterministic fake source adapters that return distinct markers; assert the exact payload sent to summary generation contains all three sections/keys and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module error propagation and graceful degradation (source adapter failure -> report assembler fallback text). Unit is too low because the bug appears in wiring/handling between components. REAL-INFRA: a controlled in-process failure from one adapter reproduces the target failure mode for this behavior (fallback vs abort), so real network is not strictly required. LLM-DEP: not about model judgment quality. MULTI-STEP: no chained agentic decisions. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text focuses on fallback behavior ("data unavailable" note) rather than real transport semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with two source adapters returning data and one adapter raising an availability error; assert job completes, output contains normal sections for available sources, and the failed section contains the `data unavailable` note.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on what the summary generator actually produces (semantic faithfulness to key values). A deterministic stub would always pass and hide real misrepresentation/hallucination failures. MOCK-HIDE: mocking summary output removes the failure class entirely. MULTI-STEP: only one model decision point (summary generation), so Workflow is not required. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes a single summary step, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real summary-generation model on representative inputs with known key metrics/issues/deploy facts; assert faithfulness criteria (key numbers/entities preserved, no contradictions) across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, exercising real HTTP/API calls plus real email transport/inbox behavior. In-process mocks can hide auth, transport, formatting, or delivery integration failures. LLM-DEP: behavior checks successful end-to-end delivery, not summary quality scoring itself; real model output quality is not the assertion focus. MULTI-STEP: not a multi-agentic pipeline requirement. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because "reaches ... inbox" requires real external infrastructure; rejected Workflow because no explicit sequential agentic decision chain is required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled script in an environment connected to real internal endpoints (or staging equivalents) and a real mailbox; verify one email arrives to configured recipients with expected subject/body sections and attachments/format intact.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff across components (email failure handling -> local persistence -> next-run retry), so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem persistence and rerun logic can be validated in-process with temp files and controlled mail-client failure; real network outage semantics are not required to catch this specific fallback bug. LLM-DEP: not dependent on model output quality. MULTI-STEP: multiple runs, but not multiple agentic decisions. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text targets fallback state machine (save + retry next run), which can be reproduced by injected delivery exceptions and two sequential job invocations.
behaviors.test_description: Run job once with email client forced to fail; assert report is written to fallback file/queue. Run job a second time with email client restored; assert persisted report is retried and marked delivered/cleared.
